_note: Slide-deck tour. intro = start lightbox. stages[] -> {name,badge,stops[]}. Each stop: title, image (ONE), caption?, lead, points[], layers[] (slugs shown), center [lng,lat] + zoom (frames the stop), or fit:'all'. cta?. 'Operating now' badge = green.

overlay.heading: Portal.Place Village: Our Story
overlay.sub: A seasonal recreational community for families, creators, and nature lovers, on 400 acres bordering Wells Gray Provincial Park near Clearwater, BC.
story: In 2021, Mike and Euvie crowdfunded this land through their podcast, Future Thinkers, to build a seasonal village centered on family, creativity, and maker culture — and our whole extended family joined us on the land.

For five years we've run it as a working RV park, campground and golf resort: 3,000 guests a season, Phase 1 complete, and the next chapter already underway — cabins, a maker space, event programs, and a community that returns every summer.
This map is where you can see it taking shape. Walk through what's built and operating, then the plan for the village — or close anytime to explore the map yourself.

stops.title: Built & Operating
stops.lead: Five seasons in — the working prototype the village builds on.
points: Full-service RV sites, tenting, bunk cabins, and a glamping dome
Private swimming lake, sauna & cold creek, shower houses, 120-person gazebo, horse corrals, golf & disc-golf course, and acres of wilderness
3,000+ visitors a year, with minimal marketing

stops.title: RV Sites
stops.lead: Full-service and power/water sites for RVs and tiny homes.
points: 45 serviced sites, plus power & water sites
Pull-through and back-in, shaded and open
The operating backbone of the campground

stops.title: Golf & Disc Golf
stops.lead: A 9-hole golf course that doubles as an 18-basket disc-golf course.
points: Golf or disc golf on the same scenic fairways
Mountain and forest views the whole way around
Open to guests and the public all season

stops.title: Glamping Dome
stops.lead: A geodesic glamping dome for unique stays.
points: Sleeps guests in comfort, open to the sky
One of the signature stays on the land

stops.title: Bunk Cabins
stops.lead: Three rental bunk cabins tucked along the creek.
points: Simple, cozy stays — among the original built accommodation
Booked through the season alongside the dome
A short walk from the lake and gathering spaces

stops.title: Sauna
stops.lead: A wood-fired cedar barrel sauna next to a cold creek.
points: Steam, then a cold dip in the creek
Part of the weekly village rhythm
A taste of the wellness spa planned for a later phase

stops.title: Community & Gathering
stops.lead: The heart of village life — where people gather, learn, and celebrate together.
points: A 120-person Event Gazebo with a kitchen for weddings, gatherings and community meetups
Forest School — outdoor learning, stories and maker days for kids 5–12 and their families
A yearly & weekly rhythm: work-stays, workshops, community sauna days, festivals & artist residencies

stops.title: Building the Village
stops.lead: The next chapters turn the working campground into a village — built in phases, funded by the Founding Members.
points: Phase 2 — Creekside cabins and a community Maker Space at the core
Phase 3 — Lakeside cabins along the shoreline and a new event gazebo
Phase 4 — Festival grounds, an outdoor event stage, and hiking & art trails

stops.title: The Maker Space
stops.lead: A community workshop — and a key element of village culture.
points: 3D printers, CNC, laser and woodworking
Fabricates cabin finishings and art installations
Used for workshops & maker meetups
notes: Remaining: build larger structure, acquire CNC

stops.title: Creekside Cabins
stops.lead: A row of small cabins set along the creek.
points: Prefab wooden cabins, a mix of rustic and modern feel
Designed for families & friends
A short walk from the campground core

stops.title: Lakeside Cabins
stops.lead: 31 cabins in two rows curving along the lake's edge.
points: 31 net-zero cabins in two rows, hugging the shoreline
Set back from the water, a short stroll to the lake
Finished in the maker space, plugged into existing services

stops.title: New Event Gazebo
stops.lead: A new pavilion for gatherings of every kind.
points: Hosts retreats, ceremonies, workshops, and more
A flexible, open community space
Part of the future phases of community buildings

stops.title: Festival Grounds
stops.lead: A leave-no-trace festival meadow — light-touch by design.
points: Clear the meadow and cut/clear the trails
Add a new shower house and grouped portapotties
Scales from 500 to 5,000 people, no permanent build
Cleared timber is milled on site for later-phase community buildings

stops.title: Event Stage
stops.lead: An outdoor stage for music, performance and festival nights.
points: A gathering point for shows, talks and celebrations
Scales up with the festival grounds
Part of the next phase of community buildings

stops.title: Hiking & Art Trails
stops.lead: An art-and-nature hike threading through the forest.
points: A trail network through the forest and along the hills
Art "landings" — installations and rest spots along the way
An immersive experience for all ages

stops.title: The Bigger Picture
stops.lead: Beyond these phases — a village, and a network.
points: An art & wellness village, educational campus, and eco-resort in later phases
Portal.Place Village is the flagship — a template to be replicated elsewhere
Part of a growing network of villages across regions
Village AI coordinating operations, logistics, and community life

overlay.heading: Founding Memberships
overlay.sub: A summer home base here for years to come.
story: We're opening a small round of Founding Memberships for people who want to make this a summer home base here for years to come. Founding Members get 1–2 weeks of free cabin or RV stays each season plus generous perks, as well as a stake in the membership company behind the village, and a voice in the future direction of the place.

The round is capped at 50 Founding Members, and we break ground once 30 are committed. We're starting with the people we already know: friends, family, past guests, and longtime podcast listeners. It's offered privately by invitation, not sold to the public, and we get to know every member personally before they join.
If that's you, we'd love to hear from you.
